name: Race along the Guadalquivir on the old path between Cordoba and Seville
name_original: Carrera por el Guadalquivir por el antiguo camino entre Córdoba y Sevilla
region_intro: Jogging around Palma del Río offers diverse landscapes, characterized by its position at the confluence of the Guadalquivir and Genil rivers, providing picturesque riverside paths. The region also features extensive orange groves in the Santa Lucía Valley, offering fragrant and pleasant running paths. Further north, the Parque Periurbano Los Cabezos presents shaded trails through Mediterranean forest, while historic irrigation canals provide long, flat routes suitable for uninterrupted running. Best jogging routes around Palma Del Río The most popular jogging route is Race along the Guadalquivir on the old path between Cordoba and Seville, an easy 2.6 miles (4.2 km) trail that takes about 30 minutes to complete. This route follows the riverbanks, offering views of the distinctive riverside landscape. Another top favourite among local runners is Iron Bridge loop from Estadio Municipal Sergio León, a moderate 5.9 miles (9.6 km) path. This loop provides a varied running experience, often completed in about 1 hour. Local runners also love the Iron Bridge – Palma del Río loop from Palma del Río, a 4.7 miles (7.5 km) trail leading through the local area, often completed in about 45 minutes. Jogging around Palma Del Río is defined by riverside paths, extensive orange groves, and trails through Mediterranean forest. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside runs to moderate loops. The routes in Palma Del Río are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from 2 reviews. More than 100 runners have used komoot to explore Palma Del Río's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Palma del Río?
A: Palma del Río offers a selection of 12 jogging routes, catering to various preferences and fitness levels. These include easy riverside paths and moderate loops through the local area.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect while jogging in Palma del Río?
A: You'll find a diverse range of landscapes. Many routes follow the banks of the Guadalquivir and Genil rivers, offering picturesque riverside views. Other paths wind through the extensive orange groves of the Santa Lucía Valley, especially fragrant during blossom season. For a different experience, you can explore trails within the Mediterranean forest of Parque Periurbano Los Cabezos.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly jogging routes in Palma del Río?
A: Yes, there are 3 easy routes suitable for beginners or those looking for a relaxed run. A great option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1001028080/race-along-the-guadalquivir-on-the-old-path-between-cordoba-and-seville" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Race along the Guadalquivir on the old path between Cordoba and Seville&lt;/a&gt;, which is 2.6 miles (4.2 km) long and follows the riverbanks.

Q: Can I find running routes with scenic views or natural features?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in natural beauty. Many routes offer views of the Guadalquivir and Genil rivers, particularly at their confluence. You can also jog through the aromatic orange groves of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4352235" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Santa Lucía valley&lt;/a&gt; or explore the Mediterranean forest in Parque Periurbano Los Cabezos, which is surrounded by the Arroyo Retortillo stream. The historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3938708" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal de los Presos del Bajo Guadalquivir&lt;/a&gt; also provides long, flat paths with views of fertile lands.

Q: Are there any circular jogging routes in Palma del Río?
A: Yes, several routes are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39837631" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iron Bridge loop from Estadio Municipal Sergio León&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 5.9 miles (9.6 km) path, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31449425" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iron Bridge loop from Palma del Río&lt;/a&gt; offers a longer 6.7 miles (10.9 km) option.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about the trails in Palma del Río?
A: The running routes in Palma del Río are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars from 2 reviews. Runners often praise the diverse terrain, from peaceful riverside paths to scenic orange groves and shaded forest trails, providing a varied and engaging experience.

Q: Are there any longer running routes for more experienced joggers?
A: Yes, for those seeking longer distances, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31449425" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iron Bridge loop from Palma del Río&lt;/a&gt; cover approximately 6.7 miles (10.9 km), offering a moderate challenge and a good workout.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Palma del Río?
A: The region is particularly pleasant during spring when the orange groves are in full bloom, offering fragrant paths. Autumn and winter also provide comfortable temperatures for running. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late evening runs are recommended during these months.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by historical sites or unique settlements?
A: While jogging, you might encounter areas known as Pagos de Huerta, which are unique historical settlements nestled among orchards, offering a glimpse into the region's agricultural past. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3938708" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal de los Presos del Bajo Guadalquivir&lt;/a&gt; is a significant historical irrigation canal that runs through the area.

Q: Can I find running routes near water bodies other than the main rivers?
A: Yes, beyond the Guadalquivir and Genil rivers, you can find routes that offer views of other water features. The Parque Periurbano Los Cabezos is surrounded by the Arroyo Retortillo stream, and nearby attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5617701" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bembézar Dam&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3415384" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñaflor Reservoir - Guadalquivir River&lt;/a&gt;, which might be accessible from some running paths.

Q: Are there any running routes that are particularly flat?
A: The paths alongside the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3938708" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal de los Presos del Bajo Guadalquivir&lt;/a&gt; are generally long and relatively flat, making them suitable for uninterrupted jogging and ideal for those preferring minimal elevation changes.
tips: Between the highway and train bridges that cross the Retortillo River, which separates Seville from Córdoba, is this ancient bridge of the Roman road that linked Córdoba with Seville, the Vía Augusta. It only preserves an arch on the other shore.

This bridge over the Guadalquivir river connects Palma del Río with its station. Its use has decreased after the construction of the Reina Sofía Bridge that crosses parallel to it and from which you have good views from here. However, in addition to going to the station, the inhabitants of Palma del Río cross the Iron Bridge every year in the pilgrimage of the Virgin of Belén, their patron saint.

waypoints.name: Roman Bridge of the Via Augusta over the Retortillo River
Iron Bridge of Palma del Río
